Home → Demo Hub → Stakeholder Presentation

Presentation mode

The 12-slide stakeholder story, rendered for the browser.

Use this page as the guided presentation surface. It includes a 30-second pitch, a 5-minute pitch, a 15-minute walkthrough, and the 12-slide structure.

Production verified through MVP-50. Runtime activation has not started.

Read-only demo

Runtime activation remains separate. This page is for stakeholder review and screen-share pacing only.

30-second pitch

The Agent Command Center is a read-only dashboard documenting 8 production-verified readiness layers from MVP-43 through MVP-50. Production verified through MVP-50. Runtime activation has not started.

5-minute pitch

Use the dashboard to show the readiness architecture, the safety wall, and the validator proof chain before any runtime is considered.

15-minute walkthrough

Use the slide sequence below to move from product framing to architecture, safety, validation, and the next-step decision.

1

Title / product identity

Purpose: Establish product identity and context. Visual: Dashboard welcome banner. Speaker note: The product is a controlled readiness architecture, not live automation.

2

Problem being solved

Purpose: Show why a single readiness surface is needed. Visual: Disconnected boxes becoming one control room. Speaker note: Stakeholders need one source of truth.

3

What the Agent Command Center is

Purpose: Define the system clearly. Key points: read-only dashboard, verified scale, operational domains, exact agent count 47,979, exact department count 1,777, traceable registry source. Visual: Dashboard with key sections highlighted.

4

What is production-verified now

Purpose: Show the current state. Key points: MVP-50, live site, all 8 readiness layers verified. Visual: MVP-50 badge.

5

The 8-layer readiness architecture

Purpose: Walk through the layers and the hierarchy behind them. Visual: Layer stack and hierarchy chain. Speaker note: Each layer proves readiness without enabling execution.

6

Safety boundaries and disabled runtime

Purpose: State what is explicitly disabled. Visual: NOT_READY_FOR_REAL_AUTOMATION badge. Speaker note: The boundary is intentional and machine-checked.

7

Live dashboard walkthrough

Purpose: Show the live site. Key points: welcome banner, current status, roadmap, archive, developer view. Visual: Annotated screenshot.

8

Validator confidence and auditability

Purpose: Show machine-checkable proof. Key points: 280 validators, dynamic latest-status, master wall, package validators, no nested E2E chains. Visual: Terminal validation output.

9

What this is not

Purpose: Prevent false assumptions. Visual: Two-column do / do-not list. Speaker note: It is not a runtime execution platform.

10

Release-readiness verdict

Purpose: State the verdict. Key points: demo readiness high, runtime readiness zero, all 8 layers verified. Visual: Verdict card.

11

Recommended next phase options

Purpose: Give stakeholders decision choices. Visual: Decision tree. Speaker note: Runtime planning is a separate phase.

12

Closing ask / feedback request

Purpose: Ask for review and feedback. Visual: Closing callout. Speaker note: Use the review questions and scorecard to structure the response.

© 2026 Devin O’Rourke. All rights reserved.

Agent Command Center, the demo pages, registry presentation, dashboard copy, visual layout, simulator materials, validator descriptions, and stakeholder presentation materials are protected project materials. No license, sale, transfer, or permission to reuse is granted by viewing this demo.

This site is a static, read-only demonstration. It does not enable live automation, command execution, database writes, Supabase mutations, deployment actions, rollback execution, alert sending, or runtime activation.

Third-party names, platforms, and services referenced for context remain the property of their respective owners.
